The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs you study, the moons you check, the matches you test. It buys nothing. It only lifts you through the six stations.

  • ☯Karmic Sage60,000

蛾 Luna Moth

0 of 13 chapters read

♎ Libra and 羊 Year of the Goat

Luna Moth

You arrive already full, even when the whole of it has to be spent before the week is out.

Zodi Animal · No. 080 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Luna Moth: Libra's exact discernment crossed with the Goat's total, unrationed devotion.

  • Libra

    gives you a discernment so exact it can tell a true match from a merely convenient one across a crowded room.

  • The Goat

    gives you a devotion with no reserve tank, total once it commits, gentle in a way that is easy to mistake for fragile.

  • The Luna Moth

    fuses both into one instinct: search precisely, then spend everything you have on exactly what you found.

One strength, one cost: the same urgency that lets you love completely, without holding anything in reserve, can also spend your whole reserve on whoever happens to be nearest when the window starts to feel like it is closing.

Meet the Luna Moth

The moth that cannot eat, and gives everything anyway.

A pale green moth of North America's deciduous forests, flying only after dark, with an adult life that lasts about a week.

Everything this reading says about you is grounded in what the luna moth actually is: an adult with no working mouth, a male built entirely around finding one female by scent, a wing pattern engineered to survive an attack rather than avoid one. This chapter is that foundation. The rest of the page draws meaning from these real traits.

You were never given more time. You were given better aim.

6 traits below

01of six

No mouth, no second chance

Animal fact

Adult luna moths have vestigial mouthparts and no functional digestive system; they never eat, running entirely on fat reserves stored during the caterpillar stage, for an adult life that lasts roughly seven to ten days.

Our reading

You arrive at anything important already carrying everything you are going to spend on it. There is no second trip back for more.

02of six

Antennae tuned to one scent

Animal fact

Male luna moths use large, feathered antennae to detect a female's pheromones from several miles away, then fly upwind through obstacles to reach the exact source of the scent.

Our reading

You sense a real connection before there is any evidence a stranger would accept. You do not follow rumor. You follow signal.

03of six

Tails that take the hit

Animal fact

The moth's long, spinning hindwing tails are believed to divert bat echolocation attacks away from the body; in trials, intact moths were caught far less often than moths with the tails removed.

Our reading

Something you carry, not the center of you, absorbs the strike that was actually aimed at your life. What looks decorative is frequently what keeps you alive.

04of six

Mates with the first to arrive

Animal fact

A female typically mates with the first male that reaches her, after which egg-laying begins the same night and continues for several nights running.

Our reading

Once the signal reads as real, you do not keep auditioning candidates. You commit to whoever actually showed up, and you rarely regret the speed of it.

05of six

Wings that need hours before flight

Animal fact

A newly emerged moth spends two to three hours pumping fluid into its small, crumpled wings before they are hard enough to fly, and cannot search for anything until that process finishes.

Our reading

You are not built to search before you are ready. The waiting looks like nothing happening. It is the whole search being made possible.

06of six

Eyespots built to unsettle

Animal fact

Each wing carries a single large eyespot, thought to startle or confuse a predator in the first instant of an encounter.

Our reading

You keep one unmistakable, watching thing turned toward whatever approaches you uninvited. It buys you exactly the moment you need.

The Luna Moth dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 080 / 144

☼ Luna Moth · Libra × Goat

The One-Week Devotion

Built for one true signal, fluent in total devotion, and honest about the week it has.

Profile

Archetype
The One-Week Devotion
Central gift
Reading a real signal from far off
Central shadow
Spending everything on whoever is nearest
Protective instinct
Commit fully, and commit fast
Growth lesson
Let the search finish first
Power phase
New Moon
Element
Earth
Ideal habitat
Dim, quiet, one true signal in the dark

These five traits are yours too. Take the test to see whether you spend yourself the way the Luna Moth spends itself, or whether you're one of the rare ones who waits for the search to finish first.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    Luna MothPick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Libra and the Goat meet

    Libra brings

    • A genuine talent for telling the right match from the merely available one
    • A preference for one true fit over several convenient options
    • An aesthetic instinct that treats even an urgent search as something worth doing well

    The Goat brings

    • Total devotion once committed, with nothing held back for later
    • A gentleness that looks fragile and is actually built entirely around care
    • A quiet, early awareness that its best windows do not last

    What the crossing makes

    Cross them and you get a creature built for total, time-limited devotion to exactly the right thing. Libra supplies the discernment to find the real signal; the Goat supplies the willingness to spend everything on it the moment it is confirmed. You would rather burn completely for something real than glow evenly for something merely convenient, and most of the time you are right to.

    Where they pull against each other

    Libra wants to be certain before it commits; the Goat feels the window closing and wants to give now. Fused well, that produces rare, accurate devotion: the search actually finishes, and then you spend everything on what it found. Fused badly, urgency wins the argument before certainty arrives, and the total giving lands on whoever was simply nearest when the clock started to feel loud. The chapters ahead are largely about telling a real signal from a near one before the giving begins.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Libra
    Goat
    50 Libra In balance Goat 50

    In balance, you let the search actually finish, confirm the signal is real, and then give everything to it without rationing. You trust your own reading enough to act on it quickly, once it is earned.

    Your element

    土

    Your element is Earth

    Earth's archetype: The Nurturer · in the Luna Moth: The Confirmed Devotion

    Earth's archetype in this system is the Nurturer. In the Luna Moth it narrows into something more specific: the Confirmed Devotion, care given completely, but only once the ground beneath it has actually been tested. Earth at its best is steady, generous, and load-bearing: the ground that lets everything else stand. In excess it becomes devotion given without verification, care poured onto whatever is closest simply because closeness felt like a claim on it. The Shadow chapter ahead wears this same signature.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal, and for the Goat that is Earth. Your Western sign adds a second note: Libra carries Air, which does not sit inside the five phases as a phase of its own but moves through all of them, the wide search that carries your Earth toward whatever it is finally going to feed. Here it means Libra supplies the range of the search, while the Goat supplies what happens once the search lands. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionCenter
    SeasonLate summer / between seasons
    VirtueFidelity
    ColorYellow

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    火Fire feeds you

    Fire feeds Earth the way warmth ripens the ground it touches. Real urgency, a genuine deadline, a person who is actually right, gives your devotion something worth spending itself on; without that heat, the giving has nowhere honest to go.

    Balances you

    木Wood checks you

    Wood breaks through Earth, and the break is not damage. A little friction, a person who makes you wait, keeps your total commitment from settling on the first ground that happened to be nearby.

    You generate

    金You feed Metal

    Earth yields Metal the way pressure over time yields something precise and hard-edged. What looks like your total giving, spent completely on the right target, is often what shapes someone else's clarity.

    You restrain

    水You hold Water

    Earth contains Water, and the containing is not control. The urgency that could otherwise flood every decision you make is exactly what your patient, deliberate Earth is built to hold at its proper level.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    Your Earth comes from one place: the Goat, whose fixed element anchors the whole Luna Moth. But Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal, and Water are not fixed labels. They are a grammar for how energy feeds and wears on itself, and the Goat's Earth is only your starting note.

    Bazi 八字, the Four Pillars, reads that grammar across your whole chart: year, month, day, and the exact hour you were born. The Luna Moth takes the element of just one pillar, your Goat year. Your full chart also carries the Air that Libra lends, in proportions only your birth time can show.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Earth in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Earth finds its closest match in the Root chakra, Muladhara, the seat of trust that there will be enough ground to land on. Its bija sound is LAM; its element there is earth.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Root chakraMuladharaBija: LAMElement: earth
    ChakraVisit your Root chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    土ElementThe Earth element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    Instinct: your first move

    You fly wide, precise circles toward the one signal that actually smells like the real thing.

    Before you have decided anything, you are already reading the air, sorting the faint true scent from the dozen near ones drifting past it. Searching is not what you do while waiting for the real thing to start. For you, the searching is already devotion, aimed and specific.

    You rarely chase what is simply close. You chase what the antennae actually confirmed, even when that means crossing a distance nobody watching would think was worth it.

    02 · Capacity

    Gift: what you do better than most

    You give everything, once the signal is confirmed, and nothing about the giving is rationed or held back.

    That same instinct, given room, becomes a rare capacity: you can sense a genuine connection from further off than almost anyone else in the room, and once you have confirmed it, you commit without the usual hedging. People who have been loved by you know exactly how they were loved. There was no ambiguity in it.

    You are also unusually present, because some part of you has always known the window is not permanent. That awareness rarely reads as anxious in you. It reads as attention, given fully, on purpose.

    03 · Defense

    Guard: what you protect, and how

    You protect what matters by giving it everything immediately, rather than testing it slowly from a safe distance.

    What the gift is guarding is the fear of running out before the real thing arrives. So you move fast once anything reads as promising, front-loading the devotion instead of rationing it across a longer, uncertain runway that might close before you got to spend it at all.

    You learned somewhere, correctly, that some windows really do not reopen. That lesson is accurate about a real signal. It becomes a liability the moment it gets applied to anyone simply standing nearby.

    04 · Distortion

    Shadow: the urgency, unconfirmed

    The total giving that once required a real signal can start attaching itself to whoever is simply near when the clock feels loud.

    What trips it. A window that feels like it is closing, a deadline, a person who is available right now, while the actual signal has not yet been confirmed.

    What your mind says. There is no time left to be sure. This has to be it.

    What you do. You commit the entire reserve to whatever is closest, and you call the proximity a sign, because waiting felt more dangerous than being wrong.

    What it costs. People learn you give completely and quickly, and some take the devotion without ever having earned the whole of it. <b>A moth that spends its one week on the nearest light instead of the true one never gets a second week to try again.</b>

    In love

    You commit fully within days of meeting someone promising, before the search has actually confirmed they are the real signal, and then wonder why the devotion outpaced the evidence.

    At work

    You throw your whole reserve at the first urgent-looking project instead of the one that actually matches your real strengths, because the urgency arrived first.

    With friends

    You give total, generous presence to whoever reaches out first, and only later notice it went to someone who was simply available, not someone who was actually right.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still give everything. Now the search finishes before the devotion begins.

    The awakened Luna Moth still flies the wide, careful search, still reads faint signals from real distance, still commits with total, undiluted devotion once it arrives. What changes is the order: confirmation now comes before the giving, not somewhere in the middle of it. Total devotion was never the problem. Aiming it at whoever was simply near was.

    You wait the one extra night the search actually needs, and the signal that survives that wait turns out to be the one worth every ounce you have.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I know the signal is real when I

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Luna Moth

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Luna Moth you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Libra Goat crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Luna Moth, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best in short, urgent, deadline-driven windows where the target is already confirmed, rather than long, ambiguous stretches with no real signal to aim at.

    01 / 05

    You lead by total, confirmed commitment

    You lead by example rather than instruction: once you have decided a direction is right, you throw your entire reserve at it, and people notice the difference between your total effort and anyone else's measured one. That intensity is contagious in short bursts.

    The failure mode is structural. If the direction was chosen under urgency rather than confirmation, your total effort gets spent defending a call that was never actually the right one, and there is no reserve left to correct course.

    02 / 05

    A confirmed target, a real deadline, permission to rest before it starts

    Clear, deadline-driven windows with an already-verified target. Time to confirm the signal before being asked to commit fully to it. A quiet stretch beforehand that lets you arrive at the work with your whole reserve intact, the way wings need hours to harden before flight.

    Environments that drain this animal: long, low-urgency stretches with no clear signal, constant reprioritization that never lets a commitment actually finish, and cultures that reward instant total buy-in over confirmed direction.

    • A confirmed target
    • Real, not manufactured, urgency
    • Recovery before the sprint
    • One clear signal, not several

    03 / 05

    Fast once confirmed, too fast when only urgent

    Your decision pattern favors speed the instant something feels real, which serves you when the signal is actually confirmed and punishes you when it was only close. Build in one mandatory night before any full commitment, no matter how loud the deadline feels.

    Your delegation pattern gives everything to the first task that reads as urgent rather than the one that is actually most important. Practice pausing to ask which target the antennae have actually confirmed before spending the whole reserve on the nearest one.

    04 / 05

    Two ways the reserve runs out too early

    The first: you are asked to commit before the search has finished, and you either freeze entirely or spend everything on the wrong target just to stop the discomfort of waiting.

    The second is quieter. You give total effort to task after task without ever confirming any of them were the real priority, and then have nothing left when the actual signal finally arrives. The burnout pattern here is not slow fatigue. It is a full reserve, spent completely, on the wrong week.

    05 / 05

    A reputation for devotion that lands where it should

    Your edge is total, confirmed commitment, and it compounds only when people learn your effort is aimed accurately, not simply available to whoever asks loudest. The long game is building a track record of total effort spent on the right target, not just any target that showed up first.

    One growth practice: before your next big commitment, name out loud what actually confirmed the signal, not just what made it feel urgent. Notice how often those are two different things.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    Dim, quiet, and built around exactly one true signal in the dark.

    The luna moth rests motionless by day, flat against bark where nothing can find it, and flies only after dark toward one confirmed scent. Its human translation: low light, few competing focal points, and a home that does not force you to search for the real thing in a room lit like an interrogation. Every recommendation below follows from that: pale green and deep indigo because Earth is your element, one warm copper touch because Fire feeds Earth, and never more than one bright signal in a room at a time.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Earth is yours; Fire is what feeds it. The bar shows roughly how much of each to use, so your one true signal always has enough warmth behind it to be worth finding.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundEarthAbout 45% of the room

    Night forest green

    A deep, dim green close to your own wing color at rest. The large, quiet surfaces a room settles around after dark.

    SupportEarthAbout 29% of the room

    Moonlit wing green

    The pale, luminous green you actually wear once your wings have finished hardening. Textiles, art, anything soft light should land on.

    AccentFireAbout 17% of the room

    Eyespot copper

    Fire feeds Earth, so this warm copper is what keeps a dim room from going cold. It is also the color of your own eyespot mark. Lamps, small pieces, the one lit thing in the room.

    SparkFireAbout 9% of the room

    Antenna brown

    Concentrated Fire, the deepest note in the room. One small touch, enough warmth to mark the true signal without lighting the whole space.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    A single-lamp, low-distraction setup with a dimmer, not an overhead flood. This animal cannot tell a real signal from a near one under constant bright light.

    Bedroom

    Pale green and deep indigo, dim and soft-lit, a room built for the kind of rest that recharges a reserve rather than merely pausing it.

    Entry

    A softly lit threshold, welcoming without being harsh, so arriving home does not feel like another search underway.

    Living area

    One warm, dimmed focal point rather than several competing bright ones, so the room itself models telling a real signal from noise.

    Night perch

    A dark, quiet corner, up off the floor, where you can rest fully motionless by day before the evening's real searching starts.

    Reflection

    A dark, quiet nook for the new-moon practice of waiting one full night before committing fully to something new.

    Elements to avoid

    • Bright, constant overhead light with no dimmer, which scatters your attention across every signal instead of the real one
    • A cluttered room with too many competing focal points, which mirrors giving yourself to whatever is nearest
    • A home with no quiet, dark corner for stillness, which removes the one place your search could actually finish
    • Any room that never fully darkens; this animal needs true dark to tell a real signal from a decoration
    The five-minute reset

    Five-minute habitat reset: dim every light in the room, sit still until your eyes adjust, and name out loud whether the thing you feel urgent about right now is a real signal or simply near.

    土 Feng Shui
    for the
    Luna Moth

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Earth element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Earth element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the one true signal

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each part of your life.

    • The lesson

      Total devotion was never the problem. Aiming it at whoever was simply near was. The search is allowed to take one more night.

    • A ritual

      At each new moon, before committing fully to something new, wait one full night in the dark and ask whether the signal is real or simply near.

    • In your space

      Keep exactly one lit, warm object in your main room, so the room itself teaches you what one true signal actually looks like.

    • In one bond

      This week, tell one person you need a night before answering, instead of responding with whatever intensity is closest to hand.

    • At work

      Before your next full-effort commitment, write down what actually confirmed it as the priority, not just what made it feel urgent.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The new moon is the darkest sky of the month, the exact condition a moth needs for a faint signal to actually be readable against the noise. It fits the Luna Moth because this is the one animal whose whole method depends on darkness doing its job properly. What to do: in the dark, before committing to anything new, wait one full night and ask whether the pull is a real signal. What to avoid: mistaking the darkness itself for the answer; it only clears the noise, it does not replace the checking.

    Your next new moon · Power Moon

    Tuesday, July 14, 2026

    4 days from today.

    When it arrives, sit in the dark you already trust and let one urgent feeling finish becoming clear before you act on it. The dark was never your enemy. Confusing nearness for certainty was.

    1. Mon Jul 13The dark before. Name one thing you have been calling urgent that has not actually been confirmed.
    2. Tue Jul 14 · New MoonThe turn. Wait the one full night. Ask whether the signal is real or simply near.
    3. Wed Jul 15The first flight. If the signal held overnight, commit to it completely, wings fully open.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    亡wáng羊yáng补bǔ牢láo

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally mend the pen after the goat has been lost

    Meaning It is not too late to fix what let something get away, as long as the mending happens before the next loss.

    A farmer loses one goat through a broken fence and is told there is still time to mend it before losing another. That is generosity most creatures are given, a second chance built into the very shape of the lesson. You were never given that shape. There is no fence to mend after your one week closes, no next loss to prevent by learning faster. So you mend in advance, checking the signal before it gets away rather than after, because you understood earlier than most that some pens only get built once.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Goat. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Luna Moth loves

    You show love with total, undiluted presence, and the risk is giving that presence to whoever asked first instead of whoever actually earned it.

    Care, for you, is total commitment: the whole reserve, spent without rationing, the moment you decide someone is real. The pattern underneath shapes every bond the same way. Trusting your own read is harder for you than giving completely once you finally trust it.

    How you show love

    With total, immediate intensity once you decide someone is the real signal, holding nothing back for a second window that may not come.

    What makes you feel safe

    A partner who lets your search actually finish instead of asking you to decide before you are sure.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your fast, total commitment can read as impulsive. It is usually the opposite: it is what happens after a long, careful search finally confirms something.

    Your conflict pattern

    You either give everything to fix it immediately or go completely still, with very little middle setting between the two.

    What to practice

    Say out loud that you need one night before responding, instead of answering with whatever intensity is nearest to hand.

    How you show up

    With rare, total presence the moment you decide someone matters, showing up completely rather than gradually.

    What they may misunderstand

    Because your devotion looks the same whether it is aimed correctly or not, people rarely notice when it landed on convenience rather than a real connection.

    What to practice

    Before offering your whole reserve to a new friendship, wait long enough to confirm it is the real signal and not simply the nearest one.

    How you show up

    With full, urgent presence during the moments that actually matter, rather than steady attention spread evenly across ordinary time.

    What they may misunderstand

    They can read your urgency as drama, when it is closer to an old, accurate sense that some windows do not stay open.

    What to practice

    Ask whether this particular family moment is a real emergency or simply nearby, before spending your whole reserve on it.

    How you show up

    As the colleague who gives total effort in short, intense windows, rather than steady, even output spread across the whole year.

    Your conflict pattern

    You spend your entire reserve defending the first direction that felt urgent, rather than checking whether it was ever actually confirmed.

    What to practice

    Ask what evidence, not just what deadline, is telling you this is the real priority.

    Healthy support, for this animal, is specific: people who let your search actually finish, who do not mistake your caution for coldness, and who are genuinely worth the whole reserve once you finally spend it on them. Choose them over the ones who simply reached out first. Total devotion is not a reward for proximity. It is what happens when the signal was real.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    火

    Pronghorn

    ♈ Aries × 马 Horse

    1942, 1954, 1966, 1978, 1990, 2002, 2014

    Secret friendsTheir Fire feeds your Earth

    The Horse is the Goat's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair. Their Fire feeds your Earth: this one is fuel.

    Read the Pronghorn

    Match II · of three

    土

    Rock Wren

    ♊ Gemini × 羊 Goat

    1943, 1955, 1967, 1979, 1991, 2003, 2015

    Same yearShared Earth

    Two Goat years run on the same clock. Two Earth natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Rock Wren

    Match III · of three

    木

    Golden Pheasant

    ♌ Leo × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Same trineWood nature

    The Rabbit shares the Goat's trine, so effort between you compounds.

    Read the Golden Pheasant

    Follow the scent trail

    Where to go from the Luna Moth

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Libra changes across the other eleven years

    You have watched Libra fuse with the Goat. The fastest way to feel the system's logic is to watch the same discernment land in eleven other bodies, most of which have far more time than one week to use it.

    All Libra animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    Nocturnal searchers with one brief window and a signal they trust from a distance: kinship, not similarity.

    Snow PetrelTaurus · Year of the Goat WallcreeperAries · Year of the Goat Sunset MothLeo · Year of the Rooster

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Slow, long-lived animals with no clock pressing on them: your pattern, inverted.

    Galápagos TortoiseTaurus · Year of the Dragon Chambered NautilusCancer · Year of the Snake

    Same year, other skies

    The Goat's total devotion under eleven other suns.

    WallcreeperAries · Year of the Goat Snow PetrelTaurus · Year of the Goat Rock WrenGemini · Year of the Goat Glasswing ButterflyCancer · Year of the Goat
    Explore all of these crossings
    Golden Brushtail PossumClark's NutcrackerSnow LeopardBar-headed GooseHercules BeetleKakapoGhost Pipefish

    Same sky, other years

    Libra's discernment wearing eleven other bodies.

    Red PandaLibra · Year of the Rat SwanLibra · Year of the Ox Arabian OryxLibra · Year of the Tiger Crowned CraneLibra · Year of the Rabbit

    Step back and see the system

    The hubs this crossing hangs from.

    The last chapter

    The Luna Moth is yours.
    Now find out who is worth flying miles for.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Luna Moth.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the new moon, your power phase, and let the search finish before you give anything away. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The One-Week Devotion · Libra × Goat · Earth 土

    Your line to keep

    I am the Luna Moth: built for one true signal, fluent in total devotion, and honest about the week it has.

    蛾

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Luna Moth now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Luna Moth wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Some people are the real signal. Some are just close enough to feel like one. The wheel tells you which.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Libra Goat shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Luna Moth before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Luna Moth · 080 / 144 · Earth 土

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Luna Moth until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Luna Moth, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Luna Moth Test

    Do you spend yourself the way the Luna Moth does?

    The Luna Moth is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Luna Moth, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Luna Moth You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here the One-Week Devotion, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • Animal Diversity Web · Actias luna
      • Barber et al. · Moth tails divert bat attack: evolution of acoustic deflection, PNAS 2015
      • University of Florida Featured Creatures · Luna moth, Actias luna

      Last reviewed 2026-07-10

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Find your Zodi Animal ✦

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.
